loopStyle: Per-turn while-loop over model calls (run_conversation: while api_call_count < max_iterations), batched concurrent/sequential tool dispatch, gated only for dangerous shell commands. A self-improving skill/memory meta-loop runs BETWEEN turns, outside this loop.

nodes.label: run_conversation(user_message)
nodes.note: **AIAgent.run_conversation** (run_agent.py:4476) delegates to the module-level **run_conversation**. It builds/restores the system prompt and may inject a **memory/skill nudge** into the turn on a turn-count interval (conversation_loop.py:553) — the only place the meta-loop touches the per-turn path.

nodes.label: Turn loop (while iterations/budget)
nodes.note: `while (api_call_count < agent.max_iterations and agent.iteration_budget.remaining > 0)`. Each iteration sanitizes the message list, builds API kwargs, calls the model, and either finishes or dispatches tools and loops. Bounded by **max_iterations** and an **iteration_budget**.

nodes.label: _interruptible_api_call
nodes.note: **agent._interruptible_api_call(api_kwargs)** runs one model generation through the active transport adapter (Anthropic / OpenAI-compatible / Bedrock / Gemini / Codex, etc.). Supports streaming via `_stream_callback`, with extensive retry/backoff and error-recovery wrapping (retry loop at conversation_loop.py:1157).

nodes.label: assistant_message.tool_calls?
nodes.note: `if assistant_message.tool_calls:` — if the turn requested tools, validate/repair the names and dispatch; otherwise the turn's text **is the final response** and the loop ends (returned at conversation_loop.py:4703).

nodes.label: _execute_tool_calls (batch)
nodes.note: **agent._execute_tool_calls** (run_agent.py:4376) runs **all** tool calls from the assistant message — independent calls **concurrently** (_execute_tool_calls_concurrent, run_agent.py:4450), otherwise **sequentially** (run_agent.py:4455). 100+ tools are available via the registry (tools/registry.py).

nodes.label: Dangerous-command guard
nodes.note: **Not a uniform per-tool gate.** Only **shell commands** flagged by **detect_dangerous_command** (tools/approval.py:482) route through `_check_all_guards` → an interactive/gateway **approval_callback** (terminal_tool.py:264). File edits and most tools run **ungated**. A frozen **HERMES_YOLO_MODE** (approval.py, read once at import to block prompt-injection escalation) and per-session/permanent allowlists can auto-approve. A low-risk command may be auto-approved by an auxiliary LLM.

nodes.label: Run tool body
nodes.note: The tool's implementation runs in the selected **terminal backend** (local / Docker / SSH / Singularity / Modal / Daytona). Output is captured as a tool result and appended to the message list for the next iteration.

nodes.label: Command blocked
nodes.note: If the user/gateway denies (or a hardline guard blocks) the dangerous command, a **`pending_approval` → blocked** result is returned instead of running it (terminal_tool.py:1929). The block message is fed back to the model as the tool result, so the loop **continues** and the model adapts.

nodes.label: Turn complete
nodes.note: When the assistant turn carried **no tool calls**, run_conversation returns the final text (conversation_loop.py:4703). The turn loop also exits on budget exhaustion or user interrupt (`_turn_exit_reason`, conversation_loop.py:733).

nodes.label: Self-improving meta-loop (between turns)
nodes.note: **Outside the per-turn loop.** On an hours-long interval gated by idle time (**curator.should_run_now**, curator.py:199; get_interval_hours/get_min_idle_hours), a **background_review** (agent/background_review.py) curates skills and memory: autonomous **skill creation** after complex tasks, skill self-improvement during use, FTS5 session search, and Honcho user modeling. This is hermes's structural signature — a second loop on a different clock — so it is represented here as one trailing node, not folded into the four turn-scoped scenarios.

scenarios.title: Model answers with **no tools**
scenarios.note: The model returns text with no `tool_calls`; tool-decision falls through and run_conversation returns the text. (A turn-count nudge may have been injected at the top, but it does not change the loop shape.)

scenarios.title: User asks to **edit a file**
scenarios.note: The model calls a file-edit tool. Crucially, hermes **does not** put file edits behind the interactive approval prompt — the dangerous-command guard targets shell commands — so dispatch routes straight to **execute** (subject to file-safety boundaries). The result is fed back, the loop re-prompts, and the model finishes.

scenarios.title: **Tool call is denied** (dangerous shell command)
scenarios.note: The model runs a flagged command (e.g. `rm -rf`). detect_dangerous_command trips, the approval callback prompts, and the user (or a hardline guard) **denies**. A blocked result is fed back, the model adapts on the next turn, and the turn ends. This is the only path that exercises hermes's gate.

scenarios.title: Model issues **several tool calls**
scenarios.note: One assistant turn carries multiple tool calls. **_execute_tool_calls** runs them in a single dispatch — independent calls **concurrently** (run_agent.py:4450). The two execute steps depict that batch; all results are appended together before the loop re-prompts and the model finishes.
